records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4115 - Muhammad ibn Qays narrated from Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as):
"Commander of the Faithful (as) ruled regarding collateral (rahn) that if its value is greater than the debt owed to the creditor and it is destroyed, the excess value must be returned to the owner of the collateral.
And if the collateral is worth less than the debt and is destroyed, the debtor must pay the remaining amount owed to the creditor.
But if the value of the collateral equals the debt, then there is no further obligation on either party."
records.arabicText: 4115 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ قَيْسٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "قَضَى أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فِي اَلرَّهْنِ إِذَا كَانَ أَكْثَرَ مِنْ مَالِ اَلْمُرْتَهِنِ فَهَلَكَ أَنْ يُؤَدَّى اَلْفَضْلُ إِلَى صَاحِبِ اَلرَّهْنِ وَ إِنْ كَانَ اَلرَّهْنُ أَقَلَّ مِنْ مَالِهِ فَهَلَكَ اَلرَّهْنُ أَدَّى إِلَيْهِ صَاحِبُهُ فَضْلَ مَالِهِ وَ إِنْ كَانَ اَلرَّهْنُ يَسْوَى مَا رَهَنَهُ فَلَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ شَيْءٌ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4115 - Muhammad ibn Qays narrated from Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Commander of the Faithful (as) ruled regarding collateral (rahn) that if its value is greater than the debt owed to the creditor and it is destroyed, the excess value must be returned to the owner of the collateral.
And if the collateral is worth less than the debt and is destroyed, the debtor must pay the remaining amount owed to the creditor.
But if the value of the collateral equals the debt, then there is no further obligation on either party."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4116 - Fadalah narrated from Aban, from Abu Abdillah (as):
"If two parties disagree about the value of collateral (rahn), with one claiming it was pledged for one thousand dirhams and the other claiming it was pledged for one hundred dirhams, the one claiming the higher value must present evidence. If he cannot provide evidence, the one claiming the lower value must take an oath."
"Similarly, if the collateral is worth either less or more than the claimed amount, and they disagree about its status, whether it is collateral (rahn) or a deposit (wadi'ah), the one claiming it is a deposit must present evidence. If he cannot provide evidence, the one claiming it is collateral must take an oath."
records.arabicText: 4116 - وَ رَوَى فَضَالَةُ عَنْ أَبَانٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "إِذَا اِخْتَلَفَا فِي اَلرَّهْنِ فَقَالَ أَحَدُهُمَا رَهَنْتَهُ بِأَلْفِ دِرْهَمٍ وَ قَالَ اَلْآخَرُ رَهَنْتُهُ بِمِائَةِ دِرْهَمٍ فَإِنَّهُ يُسْأَلُ صَاحِبُ اَلْأَلْفِ اَلْبَيِّنَةَ فَإِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ بَيِّنَةٌ حَلَفَ صَاحِبُ اَلْمِائَةِ وَ إِنْ كَانَ اَلرَّهْنُ أَقَلَّ مِمَّا رَهَنَ بِهِ أَوْ أَكْثَرَ وَ اِخْتَلَفَا فِي اَلرَّهْنِ فَقَالَ أَحَدُهُمَا هُوَ رَهْنٌ وَ قَالَ اَلْآخَرُ هُوَ وَدِيعَةٌ فَإِنَّهُ يُسْأَلُ صَاحِبُ اَلْوَدِيعَةِ اَلْبَيِّنَةَ فَإِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ بَيِّنَةٌ حَلَفَ صَاحِبُ اَلرَّهْنِ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4116 - Fadalah narrated from Aban, from Abu Abdillah (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"If two parties disagree about the value of collateral (rahn), with one claiming it was pledged for one thousand dirhams and the other claiming it was pledged for one hundred dirhams, the one claiming the higher value must present evidence. If he cannot provide evidence, the one claiming the lower value must take an oath."
"Similarly, if the collateral is worth either less or more than the claimed amount, and they disagree about its status, whether it is collateral (rahn) or a deposit (wadi'ah), the one claiming it is a deposit must present evidence. If he cannot provide evidence, the one claiming it is collateral must take an oath."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4117 - Safwan ibn Yahya narrated from Ishaq ibn Ammar, who said:
"I asked Abu Ibrahim (Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as)) about a man who pledges a slave, garment, jewelry, or household items as collateral, and the owner of the property tells the creditor, 'You are permitted to use this garment, wear it, benefit from the property, and use the servant.'
The Imam (as) replied: 'It is lawful for him if the owner permits it, but I do not like him to do so.' "
I then asked: 'What if someone pledges a house that produces income, who does the income belong to?'
Imam (as) replied: 'The income belongs to the owner of the house.' "
I further asked: 'What if someone pledges a barren land and the owner of the land tells him to cultivate it for himself?'
Imam (as) replied: 'This is lawful. It is not the same as the previous case. He cultivates it with his own money, so it is lawful for him as the owner has permitted it, because he invests his own resources and develops the land.' "
records.arabicText: 4117 - وَ رَوَى صَفْوَانُ بْنُ يَحْيَى عَنْ إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَرْهَنُ اَلْعَبْدَ أَوِ اَلثَّوْبَ أَوِ اَلْحُلِيَّ أَوْ مَتَاعَ اَلْبَيْتِ فَيَقُولُ صَاحِبُ اَلْمَتَاعِ لِلْمُرْتَهِنِ أَنْتَ فِي حِلٍّ مِنْ لُبْسِ هَذَا اَلثَّوْبِ اِلْبَسِ اَلثَّوْبَ وَ اِنْتَفِعْ بِالْمَتَاعِ وَ اِسْتَخْدِمِ اَلْخَادِمَ قَالَ "هُوَ لَهُ حَلاَلٌ إِذَا أَحَلَّهُ لَهُ وَ مَا أُحِبُّ أَنْ يَفْعَلَ" قُلْتُ فَارْتَهَنَ دَاراً لَهَا غَلَّةٌ لِمَنِ اَلْغَلَّةُ قَالَ "لِصَاحِبِ اَلدَّارِ" قُلْتُ فَارْتَهَنَ أَرْضاً بَيْضَاءَ فَقَالَ لَهُ صَاحِبُ اَلْأَرْضِ اِزْرَعْهَا لِنَفْسِكَ فَقَالَ "هَذَا حَلاَلٌ لَيْسَ هَذَا مِثْلَ هَذَا يَزْرَعُهَا بِمَالِهِ فَهُوَ لَهُ حَلاَلٌ كَمَا أَحَلَّهُ لِأَنَّهُ يَزْرَعُ بِمَالِهِ وَ يَعْمُرُهَا".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4117 - Safwan ibn Yahya narrated from Ishaq ibn Ammar, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I asked Abu Ibrahim (Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as)) about a man who pledges a slave, garment, jewelry, or household items as collateral, and the owner of the property tells the creditor, 'You are permitted to use this garment, wear it, benefit from the property, and use the servant.'
The Imam (as) replied: 'It is lawful for him if the owner permits it, but I do not like him to do so.' "
I then asked: 'What if someone pledges a house that produces income, who does the income belong to?'
Imam (as) replied: 'The income belongs to the owner of the house.' "
I further asked: 'What if someone pledges a barren land and the owner of the land tells him to cultivate it for himself?'
Imam (as) replied: 'This is lawful. It is not the same as the previous case. He cultivates it with his own money, so it is lawful for him as the owner has permitted it, because he invests his own resources and develops the land.' "

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4118 - Safwan ibn Yahya narrated from Muhammad ibn Rabah al-Qalla', who said:
"I asked Abu al-Hasan (as) about a man whose brother passed away and left behind a box containing pledged items. Some of these items had the names of their owners and the amounts for which they were pledged, while others had no information about their owners or the amounts for which they were pledged. What is the ruling regarding the items whose owners are unknown?"
Imam (as) replied: 'They are treated as part of his property.' "
records.arabicText: 4118 - وَ رَوَى صَفْوَانُ بْنُ يَحْيَى عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ رَبَاحٍ اَلْقَلاَّءِ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ هَلَكَ أَخُوهُ وَ تَرَكَ صُنْدُوقاً فِيهِ رُهُونٌ بَعْضُهَا عَلَيْهِ اِسْمُ صَاحِبِهِ وَ بِكَمْ هُوَ رُهِنَ وَ بَعْضُهَا لاَ يُدْرَى لِمَنْ هُوَ وَ لاَ بِكَمْ هُوَ رُهِنَ مَا تَرَى فِي هَذَا اَلَّذِي لاَ يُعْرَفُ صَاحِبُهُ فَقَالَ "هُوَ كَمَالِهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4118 - Safwan ibn Yahya narrated from Muhammad ibn Rabah al-Qalla', who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I asked Abu al-Hasan (as) about a man whose brother passed away and left behind a box containing pledged items. Some of these items had the names of their owners and the amounts for which they were pledged, while others had no information about their owners or the amounts for which they were pledged. What is the ruling regarding the items whose owners are unknown?"
Imam (as) replied: 'They are treated as part of his property.' "

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4119 - Abu al-Husayn Muhammad ibn Ja'far al-Asadi (may Allah (swt) be pleased with him) narrated from Musa ibn Imran al-Nakha'i, from his uncle al-Husayn ibn Yazid al-Nawfali, from Ali ibn Salim, from his father, who said:
"I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about the report that has been narrated: 'Whoever relies more on a pledge (rahn) than on his believing brother, I am free from him.'
Imam (as) replied: 'That applies when the truth becomes manifest, and our Qaim (the Mahdi Imam Muhammad ibn Hassan Al-Mahdi (as)) from among the Ahlulbayt (as) rises.'
I then asked about the report that says: 'The profit earned by a believer from another believer is riba (usury).'
Imam (as) said: 'That applies when the truth becomes manifest, and our Qaim (as) from among the Ahlulbayt (as) rises. As for today, there is no harm if a believer sells to his believing brother and earns profit from him.' "
records.arabicText: 4119 - وَ رَوَى أَبُو اَلْحُسَيْنِ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ اَلْأَسَدِيُّ رَضِيَ اَللَّهُ عَنْهُ عَنْ مُوسَى بْنِ عِمْرَانَ اَلنَّخَعِيِّ عَنْ عَمِّهِ اَلْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ يَزِيدَ اَلنَّوْفَلِيِّ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ سَالِمٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنِ اَلْخَبَرِ اَلَّذِي رُوِيَ "أَنَّ مَنْ كَانَ بِالرَّهْنِ أَوْثَقَ مِنْهُ بِأَخِيهِ اَلْمُؤْمِنِ فَأَنَا مِنْهُ بَرِيءٌ" فَقَالَ "ذَلِكَ إِذَا ظَهَرَ اَلْحَقُّ وَ قَامَ قَائِمُنَا أَهْلَ اَلْبَيْتِ " قُلْتُ فَالْخَبَرُ اَلَّذِي رُوِيَ "أَنَّ رِبْحَ اَلْمُؤْمِنِ عَلَى اَلْمُؤْمِنِ رِبًا مَا هُوَ" قَالَ "ذَاكَ إِذَا ظَهَرَ اَلْحَقُّ وَ قَامَ قَائِمُنَا أَهْلَ اَلْبَيْتِ وَ أَمَّا اَلْيَوْمَ فَلاَ بَأْسَ بِأَنْ يَبِيعَ مِنَ اَلْأَخِ اَلْمُؤْمِنِ وَ يَرْبَحَ عَلَيْهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4119 - Abu al-Husayn Muhammad ibn Ja'far al-Asadi (may Allah (swt) be pleased with him) narrated from Musa ibn Imran al-Nakha'i, from his uncle al-Husayn ibn Yazid al-Nawfali, from Ali ibn Salim, from his father, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about the report that has been narrated: 'Whoever relies more on a pledge (rahn) than on his believing brother, I am free from him.'
Imam (as) replied: 'That applies when the truth becomes manifest, and our Qaim (the Mahdi Imam Muhammad ibn Hassan Al-Mahdi (as)) from among the Ahlulbayt (as) rises.'
I then asked about the report that says: 'The profit earned by a believer from another believer is riba (usury).'
Imam (as) said: 'That applies when the truth becomes manifest, and our Qaim (as) from among the Ahlulbayt (as) rises. As for today, there is no harm if a believer sells to his believing brother and earns profit from him.' "

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4120 - Al-Alaa narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, who reported from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as):
"I asked Imam (as) about a man who pledges his bondwoman (as collateral). Is it permissible for him to have intercourse with her?"
Imam (as) replied: "Those who have taken her as collateral (the creditors) prevent him from having access to her."
I then asked: "What if he manages to be with her privately without the knowledge of those who took her as collateral?"
Imam (as) said: "Yes, I do not see any harm in this."
records.arabicText: 4120 - وَ رَوَى اَلْعَلاَءُ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَرْهَنُ جَارِيَتَهُ أَ يَحِلُّ لَهُ أَنْ يَطَأَهَا قَالَ "إِنَّ اَلَّذِينَ اِرْتَهَنُوهَا يَحُولُونَ بَيْنَهُ وَ بَيْنَهَا" قُلْتُ أَ رَأَيْتَ إِنْ قَدَرَ عَلَيْهَا خَالِياً وَ لَمْ يَعْلَمِ اَلَّذِينَ اِرْتَهَنُوهَا قَالَ "نَعَمْ لاَ أَرَى بِهَذَا بَأْساً".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4120 - Al-Alaa narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, who reported from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I asked Imam (as) about a man who pledges his bondwoman (as collateral). Is it permissible for him to have intercourse with her?"
Imam (as) replied: "Those who have taken her as collateral (the creditors) prevent him from having access to her."
I then asked: "What if he manages to be with her privately without the knowledge of those who took her as collateral?"
Imam (as) said: "Yes, I do not see any harm in this."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Allah (swt), the Blessed and Exalted, said:
"They ask you what has been made lawful for them. Say: Lawful to you are all good things, and what you have taught of hunting animals, trained as hounds, teaching them of what Allah (swt) has taught you. So eat of what they catch for you, and mention the name of Allah (swt) upon it." (Surah Al-Ma'idah 5:4).
records.arabicText: قَالَ اللَّهُ تَبَارَكَ وَ تَعَالَى ۝ يَسْئَلُونَكَ ما ذا أُحِلَّ لَهُمْ‌ قُلْ أُحِلَّ لَكُمُ الطَّيِّباتُ وَ ما عَلَّمْتُمْ مِنَ الْجَوارِحِ‌ مُكَلِّبِينَ‌ تُعَلِّمُونَهُنَ‌ مِمَّا عَلَّمَكُمُ اللَّهُ‌ فَكُلُوا مِمَّا أَمْسَكْنَ عَلَيْكُمْ‌ وَ اذْكُرُوا اسْمَ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ‌  ۝

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4121 - Musa ibn Bakr narrated from Zurara, from Abu Abdullah (as), that he said:
"Regarding the hunting of a dog, if its owner releases it and pronounces the name of Allah (swt), then he may eat whatever it catches for him, even if it kills. And if it eats from it, then eat what remains.
If the dog is not trained, but the owner trains it at the moment he releases it, he may eat from it, for it is considered trained.
However, apart from dogs, such as animals hunted by cheetahs, falcons, and similar creatures, do not eat from their catch you reach it and perform the proper slaughtering (dhabh). This is because Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, said: 'Mukallibeen' (those who train hunting dogs)
Thus, apart from dogs, their catch is not lawful to eat unless you reach it and slaughter it properly."
records.arabicText: 4121 - وَ رَوَى مُوسَى بْنُ بَكْرٍ عَنْ زُرَارَةَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : "فِي صَيْدِ اَلْكَلْبِ إِنْ أَرْسَلَهُ صَاحِبُهُ وَ سَمَّى فَلْيَأْكُلْ كُلَّ مَا أَمْسَكَ عَلَيْهِ وَ إِنْ قَتَلَ وَ إِنْ أَكَلَ فَكُلْ مَا بَقِيَ وَ إِنْ كَانَ غَيْرَ مُعَلَّمٍ فَعَلَّمَهُ سَاعَتَهُ حِينَ يُرْسِلُهُ فَلْيَأْكُلْ مِنْهُ فَإِنَّهُ مُعَلَّمٌ فَأَمَّا مَا خَلاَ اَلْكِلاَبَ مِمَّا تَصِيدُهُ اَلْفُهُودُ وَ اَلصُّقُورُ وَ أَشْبَاهُهُ فَلاَ تَأْكُلْ مِنْ صَيْدِهِ إِلاَّ مَا أَدْرَكْتَ ذَكَاتَهُ، لِأَنَّ اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ قَالَ "مُكَلِّبِينَ" فَمَا خَلاَ اَلْكِلاَبَ فَلَيْسَ صَيْدُهُ بِالَّذِي يُؤْكَلُ إِلاَّ أَنْ تُدْرِكَ ذَكَاتَهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4121 - Musa ibn Bakr narrated from Zurara, from Abu Abdullah (as), that he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Regarding the hunting of a dog, if its owner releases it and pronounces the name of Allah (swt), then he may eat whatever it catches for him, even if it kills. And if it eats from it, then eat what remains.
If the dog is not trained, but the owner trains it at the moment he releases it, he may eat from it, for it is considered trained.
However, apart from dogs, such as animals hunted by cheetahs, falcons, and similar creatures, do not eat from their catch you reach it and perform the proper slaughtering (dhabh). This is because Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, said: 'Mukallibeen' (those who train hunting dogs)
Thus, apart from dogs, their catch is not lawful to eat unless you reach it and slaughter it properly."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4122 - And in another narration, Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Eat whatever the dog has eaten from, even if it has eaten two-thirds of it. Eat whatever the dog has eaten, even if nothing remains of it except a single piece."
records.arabicText: 4122 - وَ فِي خَبَرٍ آخَرَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "كُلْ مَا أَكَلَ مِنْهُ اَلْكَلْبُ وَ إِنْ أَكَلَ مِنْهُ ثُلُثَيْهِ كُلْ مَا أَكَلَ اَلْكَلْبُ وَ إِنْ لَمْ يَبْقَ مِنْهُ إِلاَّ بَضْعَةٌ وَاحِدَةٌ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4122 - And in another narration, Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Eat whatever the dog has eaten from, even if it has eaten two-thirds of it. Eat whatever the dog has eaten, even if nothing remains of it except a single piece."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4123 - Hisham bin Salim narrated from Sulaiman bin Khalid, who said:
"I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a Magian's dog that is taken by a Muslim man, and he pronounces the name of Allah (swt) when releasing it. Should he eat what the dog catches for him?"
Imam (as) said: 'Yes, because it is trained and the name of Allah (swt) has been mentioned upon it.'"
records.arabicText: 4123 - وَ رَوَى هِشَامُ بْنُ سَالِمٍ عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ خَالِدٍ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ كَلْبِ اَلْمَجُوسِيِّ يَأْخُذُهُ اَلرَّجُلُ اَلْمُسْلِمُ فَيُسَمِّي حِينَ يُرْسِلُهُ أَ يَأْكُلُ مَا أَمْسَكَ عَلَيْهِ قَالَ "نَعَمْ لِأَنَّهُ مُكَلَّبٌ وَ ذَكَرَ اِسْمَ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4123 - Hisham bin Salim narrated from Sulaiman bin Khalid, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a Magian's dog that is taken by a Muslim man, and he pronounces the name of Allah (swt) when releasing it. Should he eat what the dog catches for him?"
Imam (as) said: 'Yes, because it is trained and the name of Allah (swt) has been mentioned upon it.'"

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4124 - Al-Nadr bin Suwayd narrated from Al-Qasim bin Sulaiman, who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a dog that escaped and was not released by its owner, yet it hunted and its owner found it after it had killed the prey. Should he eat from it?
Imam (as) said: 'No. If it hunted while the owner had pronounced the name of Allah (swt), then he may eat from it.
But if it hunted without the name of Allah (swt) being mentioned, then he should not eat from it. And this is from what Allah (swt) has said:
"Of what you have trained among hunting animals, teaching them as Allah (swt) has taught you" (Surah Al-Ma'idah 5:4)
records.arabicText: 4124 - وَ رَوَى اَلنَّضْرُ بْنُ سُوَيْدٍ عَنِ اَلْقَاسِمِ بْنِ سُلَيْمَانَ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ كَلْبٍ أَفْلَتَ وَ لَمْ يُرْسِلْهُ صَاحِبُهُ فَصَادَ فَأَدْرَكَهُ صَاحِبُهُ وَ قَدْ قَتَلَهُ أَ يَأْكُلُ مِنْهُ فَقَالَ "لاَ إِذَا صَادَ وَ قَدْ سَمَّى فَلْيَأْكُلْ وَ إِذَا صَادَ وَ لَمْ يُسَمِّ فَلاَ يَأْكُلْ وَ هُوَ مِمَّا ۝ عَلَّمْتُمْ مِنَ اَلْجَوٰارِحِ مُكَلِّبِينَ ۝ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4124 - Al-Nadr bin Suwayd narrated from Al-Qasim bin Sulaiman, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a dog that escaped and was not released by its owner, yet it hunted and its owner found it after it had killed the prey. Should he eat from it?
Imam (as) said: 'No. If it hunted while the owner had pronounced the name of Allah (swt), then he may eat from it.
But if it hunted without the name of Allah (swt) being mentioned, then he should not eat from it. And this is from what Allah (swt) has said:
"Of what you have trained among hunting animals, teaching them as Allah (swt) has taught you" (Surah Al-Ma'idah 5:4)

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4125 - Musa bin Bakr narrated from Zurara, who reported from Abu Abdullah (as):
"If a man sends his hunting dog and forgets to mention the name of Allah (swt), it is like one who slaughters an animal and forgets to mention the name of Allah (swt).
The same applies if he shoots (an arrow) and forgets to mention the name of Allah (swt)."
records.arabicText: 4125 - وَ رَوَى مُوسَى بْنُ بَكْرٍ عَنْ زُرَارَةَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "إِذَا أَرْسَلَ اَلرَّجُلُ كَلْبَهُ وَ نَسِيَ أَنْ يُسَمِّيَ فَهُوَ بِمَنْزِلَةِ مَنْ قَدْ ذَبَحَ وَ نَسِيَ أَنْ يُسَمِّيَ وَ كَذَلِكَ إِذَا رَمَى وَ نَسِيَ أَنْ يُسَمِّيَ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4125 - Musa bin Bakr narrated from Zurara, who reported from Abu Abdullah (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"If a man sends his hunting dog and forgets to mention the name of Allah (swt), it is like one who slaughters an animal and forgets to mention the name of Allah (swt).
The same applies if he shoots (an arrow) and forgets to mention the name of Allah (swt)."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4126 - And the ruling regarding that, as mentioned in another narration, is:
"He should mention the name of Allah (swt) at the time of eating."
records.arabicText: 4126 - وَ حُكْمُ ذَلِكَ فِي خَبَرٍ آخَرَ "أَنْ يُسَمِّيَ حِينَ يَأْكُلُ".

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4127 - Hammad ibn Isa narrated from Hariz who said:
Abu Abdullah (as), was asked about an animal shot with an arrow, and its owner finds it the next day. Should he eat from it?
Imam (as) said: "If he knows that his shot is what killed it, then he may eat it, provided that he had mentioned the name of Allah (swt) (at the time of shooting)."
records.arabicText: 4127 - وَ رَوَى حَمَّادُ بْنُ عِيسَى عَنْ حَرِيزٍ قَالَ : سُئِلَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنِ اَلرَّمِيَّةِ يَجِدُهَا صَاحِبُهَا مِنَ اَلْغَدِ أَ يَأْكُلُ مِنْهَا قَالَ "إِنْ كَانَ يَعْلَمُ أَنَّ رَمْيَتَهُ هِيَ قَتَلَتْهُ فَلْيَأْكُلْ وَ ذَلِكَ إِذَا كَانَ قَدْ سَمَّى".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4127 - Hammad ibn Isa narrated from Hariz who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
Abu Abdullah (as), was asked about an animal shot with an arrow, and its owner finds it the next day. Should he eat from it?
Imam (as) said: "If he knows that his shot is what killed it, then he may eat it, provided that he had mentioned the name of Allah (swt) (at the time of shooting)."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4128 - Aban narrated from Abdur-Rahman ibn Abi Abdullah who said:
Abu Abdullah (as), said: "Whatever is caught by a snare and has part of it severed, then it is considered dead. However, if you find the rest of its body alive, then perform the proper slaughtering (Zabiha) and eat from it."
records.arabicText: 4128 - وَ رَوَى أَبَانٌ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَلرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "مَا أَخَذَتِ اَلْحِبَالَةُ وَ قَطَعَتْ مِنْهُ فَهُوَ مَيْتَةٌ وَ مَا أَدْرَكْتَ مِنْ سَائِرِ جَسَدِهِ حَيّاً فَذَكِّهِ ثُمَّ كُلْ مِنْهُ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.4128 - Aban narrated from Abdur-Rahman ibn Abi Abdullah who said:
Abu Abdullah (as), said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whatever is caught by a snare and has part of it severed, then it is considered dead. However, if you find the rest of its body alive, then perform the proper slaughtering (Zabiha) and eat from it."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4129 - Aban ibn Uthman narrated from Isa al-Qummi who said:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as): "I shoot an arrow, but I do not know whether I mentioned the name of Allah (swt) (Bismillah) or not."
Imam (as) said: "Eat, and there is no problem."
I said: "I shoot, and it disappears from my sight, then I find my arrow in it."
Imam (as) said: "Eat as long as nothing has been eaten from it. But if something has been eaten from it, then do not eat it."
records.arabicText: 4129 - وَ رَوَى أَبَانُ بْنُ عُثْمَانَ عَنْ عِيسَى اَلْقُمِّيِّ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَرْمِي بِسَهْمٍ فَلاَ أَدْرِي أَ سَمَّيْتُ أَمْ لَمْ أُسَمِّ فَقَالَ "كُلْ وَ لاَ بَأْسَ " فَقُلْتُ أَرْمِي فَيَغِيبُ عَنِّي فَأَجِدُ سَهْمِي فِيهِ فَقَالَ "كُلْ مَا لَمْ يُؤْكَلْ مِنْهُ وَ إِنْ أُكِلَ مِنْهُ فَلاَ تَأْكُلْ" مِنْهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4129 - Aban ibn Uthman narrated from Isa al-Qummi who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as): "I shoot an arrow, but I do not know whether I mentioned the name of Allah (swt) (Bismillah) or not."
Imam (as) said: "Eat, and there is no problem."
I said: "I shoot, and it disappears from my sight, then I find my arrow in it."
Imam (as) said: "Eat as long as nothing has been eaten from it. But if something has been eaten from it, then do not eat it."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4130 - Muhammad ibn Ali al-Halabi asked Imam (as) about hunting when a man strikes the prey with his sword, pierces it with his spear, or shoots it with his arrow, and he had mentioned the name of Allah (swt) (Bismillah) at the time of doing so.
Imam (as) said: "Eat it, and there is no problem with it."
records.arabicText: 4130 - وَ سَأَلَهُ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ اَلْحَلَبِيُّ : عَنِ اَلصَّيْدِ يَضْرِبُهُ اَلرَّجُلُ بِالسَّيْفِ أَوْ يَطْعُنُهُ بِرُمْحِهِ أَوْ يَرْمِيهِ بِسَهْمِهِ فَيَقْتُلُهُ وَ قَدْ سَمَّى حِينَ فَعَلَ ذَلِكَ قَالَ "كُلْهُ فَلاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.4130 - Muhammad ibn Ali al-Halabi asked Imam (as) about hunting when a man strikes the prey with his sword, pierces it with his spear, or shoots it with his arrow, and he had mentioned the name of Allah (swt) (Bismillah) at the time of doing so.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Eat it, and there is no problem with it."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4131 - Ibn Muskan narrated from al-Halabi who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as), about a man who shoots an arrow at a game animal, and it strikes it sideways and kills it. He had mentioned the name of Allah (swt) (Bismillah) when he shot the arrow, but the arrowhead did not wound it.
Imam (as) said: "If the arrow that struck it is what killed it, then when he finds it, let him eat it."
records.arabicText: 4131 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ مُسْكَانَ عَنِ اَلْحَلَبِيِّ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنِ اَلصَّيْدِ يَرْمِيهِ اَلرَّجُلُ بِسَهْمٍ فَيُصِيبُهُ مُعْتَرِضاً فَيَقْتُلُهُ وَ قَدْ سَمَّى عَلَيْهِ حِينَ رَمَى وَ لَمْ تُصِبْهُ اَلْحَدِيدَةُ فَقَالَ "إِنْ كَانَ اَلسَّهْمُ اَلَّذِي أَصَابَهُ هُوَ قَتَلَهُ فَإِذَا رَآهُ فَلْيَأْكُلْهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.4131 - Ibn Muskan narrated from al-Halabi who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as), about a man who shoots an arrow at a game animal, and it strikes it sideways and kills it. He had mentioned the name of Allah (swt) (Bismillah) when he shot the arrow, but the arrowhead did not wound it.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "If the arrow that struck it is what killed it, then when he finds it, let him eat it."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4132 - Zurara heard Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), saying:
"There is no harm in what the 'mi'raad' (a hunting tool) kills, as long as it is specifically made for that purpose."
records.arabicText: 4132 - وَ سَمِعَ زُرَارَةُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَقُولُ : "فِيمَا قَتَلَ اَلْمِعْرَاضُ لاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ إِذَا كَانَ إِنَّمَا يُصْنَعُ لِذَلِكَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4132 - Zurara heard Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), saying:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no harm in what the 'mi'raad' (a hunting tool) kills, as long as it is specifically made for that purpose."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4133 - In a narration from Hammad, from Al-Halabi, from Abu Abdullah (as):
Imam (as) was asked about what the 'mi'raad' (a hunting tool) strikes down from game.
Imam (as) said: "If he has no other arrows except the 'Mi'rad' and mentioned the name of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, over it, then he may eat from what it killed. However, if he had other arrows besides it, then he should not."
records.arabicText: 4133 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ حَمَّادٍ عَنِ اَلْحَلَبِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : أَنَّهُ سُئِلَ عَمَّا صَرَعَ اَلْمِعْرَاضُ مِنَ اَلصَّيْدِ فَقَالَ "إِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ نَبْلٌ غَيْرُ اَلْمِعْرَاضِ وَ ذَكَرَ اِسْمَ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ عَلَيْهِ فَلْيَأْكُلْ مِمَّا قَتَلَ وَ إِنْ كَانَ لَهُ نَبْلٌ غَيْرُهُ فَلاَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4133 - In a narration from Hammad, from Al-Halabi, from Abu Abdullah (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
Imam (as) was asked about what the 'mi'raad' (a hunting tool) strikes down from game.
Imam (as) said: "If he has no other arrows except the 'Mi'rad' and mentioned the name of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, over it, then he may eat from what it killed. However, if he had other arrows besides it, then he should not."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4134 - And Commander of the Faithful, peace be upon him, used to say:
"If that is his weapon which he uses to hunt with, then there is no harm."
records.arabicText: 4134 - وَ كَانَ أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَقُولُ : "إِذَا كَانَ ذَلِكَ سِلاَحَهُ اَلَّذِي يَرْمِي بِهِ فَلاَ بَأْسَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4134 - And Commander of the Faithful, peace be upon him, used to say:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If that is his weapon which he uses to hunt with, then there is no harm."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4135 - And in another report: "If that is his shooting instrument, then there is no harm."
records.arabicText: 4135 - وَ فِي خَبَرٍ آخَرَ : "إِنْ كَانَتْ تِلْكَ مِرْمَاتَهُ فَلاَ بَأْسَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4135 - And in another report:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If that is his shooting instrument, then there is no harm."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4136 - And it was narrated:
"If it pierced, it may be eaten, but if it did not pierce, it may not be eaten."
records.arabicText: 4136 - وَ رُوِيَ : "أَنَّهُ إِنْ خَرَقَ أُكِلَ وَ إِنْ لَمْ يَخْرِقْ لَمْ يُؤْكَلْ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4136 - And it was narrated:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If it pierced, it may be eaten, but if it did not pierce, it may not be eaten."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4137 - Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said regarding a man who has arrows without iron tips, and they are entirely wooden. If he shoots with the wooden arrow and strikes the bird in the middle while it is flying and kills it, and he mentions the name of Allah (swt) upon it, even if no blood comes out, and these are known arrows, then he may eat from it if he has mentioned the name of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic.
records.arabicText: 4137 - وَ قَالَ عَلِيٌّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "فِي رَجُلٍ لَهُ نِبَالٌ لَيْسَ فِيهَا حَدِيدٌ وَ هِيَ عِيدَانٌ كُلُّهَا فَيَرْمِي بِالْعُودِ فَيُصِيبُ وَسَطَ اَلطَّيْرِ مُعْتَرِضاً فَيَقْتُلُهُ وَ يَذْكُرُ اِسْمَ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ وَ إِنْ لَمْ يَخْرُجْ دَمٌ وَ هِيَ نِبَالَةٌ مَعْلُومَةٌ فَيَأْكُلُ مِنْهُ إِذَا ذَكَرَ اِسْمَ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4137 - Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said regarding a man who has arrows without iron tips, and they are entirely wooden.
records.thaqalaynMatn: If he shoots with the wooden arrow and strikes the bird in the middle while it is flying and kills it, and he mentions the name of Allah (swt) upon it, even if no blood comes out, and these are known arrows, then he may eat from it if he has mentioned the name of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4138 - Hammad ibn Uthman narrated from Al-Halabi, and Hammad ibn Isa narrated from Hariz, from Abu Abdillah (as), that he was asked about killing an animal with a stone or a bullet (projectile).
Imam (as) said: "No, it should not be eaten."
records.arabicText: 4138 - وَ رَوَى حَمَّادُ بْنُ عُثْمَانَ عَنِ اَلْحَلَبِيِّ وَ حَمَّادُ بْنُ عِيسَى عَنْ حَرِيزٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : أَنَّهُ سُئِلَ عَنْ قَتْلِ اَلْحَجَرِ وَ اَلْبُنْدُقِ أَ يُؤْكَلُ فَقَالَ "لاَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4138 - Hammad ibn Uthman narrated from Al-Halabi, and Hammad ibn Isa narrated from Hariz, from Abu Abdillah (as), that he was asked about killing an animal with a stone or a bullet (projectile).
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "No, it should not be eaten."
